title: Little Yeldham Wellington Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Plt Off. Charles Henry Gale

external_narratives.summary: Records Wellington HF471 of 30 OTU as having crashed during a training flight. One crew member, Sgt Ronald Victor Smith (aged 21), was the wireless operator/air gunner and was buried at Little Yeldham Churchyard, Essex -- suggesting the aircraft crashed in or near the village. Plt Off Charles Henry Gale received his commission on 6 April 1944, shortly before the fatal flight.
key_details: Sgt Ronald Victor Smith aged 21, buried Little Yeldham Churchyard
Gale commissioned 6 April 1944, days before crash

external_narratives.summary: The Midlands Aircraft Recovery Group's 1944 crash list includes Wellington HF471 among the training losses from 30 OTU at RAF Hixon in Staffordshire. The unit suffered significant attrition during the training programme, with crashes occurring across a wide area of central and eastern England.
key_details: 30 OTU based at RAF Hixon, Staffordshire

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
